title: alectra alba
intro: Alectra alba, also known as White Thread, is a plant species that belongs to the family Orobanchaceae, and is a type of parasitic plant that obtains its nutrients by connecting to the roots of host plants. The medicinal properties of Alectra alba include its use in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Alectra alba include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been shown to help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and wounds. In herbal preparations, Alectra alba is often dried and made into a powder, which can be added to teas or used in topical applications to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
